items.title: Confusion matrix counts
items.description: TP, FP, TN, and FN at a fixed threshold are the inputs to precision and recall. Define and read the 2×2 layout in the Confusion Matrix topic before computing rates here.

items.title: Regression vs classification
items.description: Precision, recall, and F1 apply to discrete class predictions, not continuous regression targets. Classification tasks with labeled outcomes are the prerequisite problem framing.

items.title: Hypothesis testing mindset
items.description: Choosing precision vs recall mirrors choosing α (false positive rate) vs power (1 − β) in hypothesis testing—both encode how much false alarms vs missed detections you tolerate when evidence is noisy.

items.title: LLM evaluation techniques
items.description: LLM judges and automated graders often report precision/recall-style agreement on rubric items (e.g. hallucination detected, policy violation flagged). The same trade-off between false alarms and missed failures appears in generative evaluation pipelines.
